*** 1 INJ *** AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 532 IN COVINGTON COUNTY AND TRACKED NORTHEAST. IT CAUSED MINOR TREE DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH. IT BECAME ITS STRONGEST AS IT APPROACHED HWY 37 NEAR J. NELSON DRIVE AND JEWEL OWENS LANE. AT THIS LOCATION, IT UPROOTED AND SNAPPED SEVERAL LARGE TREES AND DAMAGED A FEW STRUCTURES, INCLUDING HOMES. THE TORNADO CONTINUED INTO NORTHWEST JONES COUNTY WHERE A DOUBLE WIDE MOBILE HOME WAS PUSHED OFF OF ITS FOUNDATION AND A FEW SHEDS AND A BARN WAS DESTROYED ON CARTER DEES ROAD. AN OCCUPANT OF THE MOBILE HOME SUFFERED A MINOR INJURY. THE TORNADO LIFTED SOON AFTER THIS DAMAGE OCCURRED. MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS: 100 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH: 250 YARDS. PATH LENGTH: 7.51 MILES. 1 MINOR INJURY OCCURRED IN A MOBILE HOME.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 95 MPH AND PATH WIDTH OF 400 YARDS, CONTINUED INTO SOUTHWEST RICHLAND PARISH FROM HEBERT IN CALDWELL PARISH. THE TORNADO CROSSED THE SOUTH END OF WOOLEN LAKE AS IT ENDED. THE PATH LENGTH IN RICHLAND PARISH WAS 1.8 MILES, AND THE TOTAL PATH LENGTH IS STILL TO BE DETERMINED. MOST DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH OF THIS TORNADO WAS THE RESULT OF SNAPPED OR UPROOTED TREES, WITH SOME HOMES, SHEDS, AND CAMPERS SUSTAINING DAMAGE AS A RESULT. IN ADDITION, THE SKIRTING AND DECK ON A MOBILE HOME WERE DAMAGED AND A FENCE WAS BLOWN OVER.

*** 4 INJ *** AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 105 MPH, A PATH LENGTH OF 4.2 MILES, AND PATH WIDTH OF 650 YARDS, BEGAN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF BILL TAYLOR RD AND AUGUSTUS RD. IT CONTINUED ENE ACROSS MIDDLE RD, NEWLIGHT RD, AND CROSSED INTO FRANKLIN PARISH, ENDING JUST PAST US 425. A MOBILE HOME WAS ROLLED ON ITS SIDE AND ON TOP OF A VEHICLE. FOUR OCCUPANTS OF THE HOME SUSTAINED MINOR INJURIES. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. A FEW OTHER HOMES SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE TO ROOFS, SIDING, AND SKIRTING.

THIS TORNADO BEGAN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF DENDY ROAD AND MS HIGHWAY 413 WHERE IT SNAPPED MULTIPLE LARGE TREE LIMBS. THE TORNADO PROCEEDED NORTHEAST TOWARD THE NATCHEZ TRACE WHERE IT FIRST CROSSED WHITES ROAD AND UPROOTED SEVERAL TREES. THE STRONGEST WINDS OCCURRED AS THE TORNADO PASSED OVER MILLER AND RABUN ROADS AND CONTINUED ALONG THE NATCHEZ TRACE PARKWAY NEAR STEWART WEIR ROAD AND HAMRICK ROAD. NUMEROUS LARGE SOFT AND HARDWOOD TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED THROUGH THIS AREA WHERE WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 110MPH. THE TORNADO CONTINUED ALONG THE NATCHEZ TRACE TO NEAR REX ROAD CAUSING SPORADIC TREE DAMAGE WITH A FEW TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. THE TORNADO QUICKLY WEAKENED AND DISSIPATED OVER A WOODED AREA SOUTH OF THE NATCHEZ TRACE PARKWAY BUT BEFORE REACHING CHESTER TOMNOLEN ROAD.

IT CAUSED STRUCTURE DAMAGE TO A FEW BUILDINGS ALONG 14TH STREET, INCLUDING ROOF AND FAÃ§ADE DAMAGE. THE TORNADO REACHED ITS STRONGEST INTENSITY OF EF2 STRENGTH AS IT NEARED 23RD STREET. HERE IT SNAPPED A COUPLE OF POWER POLES, ALONG WITH MULTIPLE HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES. IN ADDITION, IT TORE THE ROOF OFF OF AN APARTMENT BUILDING AND A PORTION OF A BUILDING AT MAGNOLIA MIDDLE SCHOOL. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR WILLOWLAKE ROAD.
